name: Russian
register: Reverent contemporary Russian Bible register in the wording family of the Синодальный перевод (Synodal Translation, 1876) — the most-trusted and most-loved Russian Bible across Orthodox, Baptist, and Evangelical readers — with lightly modernized orthography and a warm, personal address in the filial passages. Where Synodal wording is archaic (e.g. «Дух Святый»), the config uses the modern nominative form Russian believers now read and pray.
quote_convention: Primary quotation uses guillemets «…»; nested/inner quotes use low-high German-style „…“. Dialogue in narrative may also use em-dash openers (— …), as in Russian literary prose.
poetry_convention: The Synodal tradition historically set poetry as running prose; contemporary reverent editions (Russian Bible Society) break Psalms, prophets, and canticles into indented poetic lines with a hanging indent marking each parallel colon. FHB follows the indented-parallel-line convention, one line per Hebrew colon.

glossary.note: Divine name. Built on the filial Отец, not Бог; Небесный is the standard Synodal adjective for the Father in heaven (cf. «Отец ваш Небесный», Matt 6). Contains the Отец stem for conformance.

Primary filial divine name (standing/nominative form). In direct address the vocative «Отче наш» (Lord's Prayer, Matt 6:9) is used. NEVER revert to Бог or Господь — the whole filial vision rests on this word.
Filial definite name. Russian has no article, so the capitalized bare Отец carries the definiteness; distinguished from generic Бог. Keeps the Отец stem.
Filial divine name without the possessive. Word order Небесный Отец reads as a warm title; Отец Небесный is an acceptable liturgical variant. Never Бог.
Aramaic address retained exactly as in Synodal (Mark 14:36; Rom 8:15; Gal 4:6), followed by the Russian vocative Отче as the built-in gloss. The Отч- stem satisfies conformance.
REVERSION TOKEN — the non-filial contrast word. Traditional Synodal term for God-as-God. Use ONLY where the English keeps 'God' as deity; never as a substitute for Отец наш.

REVERSION TOKEN — Synodal renders the Tetragrammaton YHWH as Господь. NOTE for reviewer: unlike Telugu (యెహోవా vs ప్రభువు), Russian uses the same word Господь for the covenant LORD and for 'Lord' addressed to Jesus; FHB may distinguish the covenant name with small caps ГОСПОДЬ. Never a substitute for Отец наш.
Modernized from archaic Synodal «Дух Святый» to the form contemporary readers use; Дух Святой is an equal-register variant.
Relational (of the Father), NOT the by-identity title Святой Дух. Follows Synodal «Дух Отца вашего» (Matt 10:20), shifted to first person. Carries the Отц- stem.
Fixed Synodal Christological title; unchanged.
The Son (Jesus). Capitalized bare Сын carries definiteness; correlate of the filial Отец.

Traditional Synodal title; 'God' is retained here as God-as-God (reversion-token logic), so the standard Сын Божий stands. REVIEWER TO CONFIRM whether FHB's filial vision prefers «Сын Отца» (Son of the Father), paralleling Telugu తండ్రి కుమారుడు.

From Synodal «Сына Своего Единородного» (John 3:16). Единородный is the settled, beloved rendering of monogenēs; modern «единственный Сын» is a plainer alternative if the reviewer wants lower register.
FHB filial rendering of 'kingdom of God': Synodal «Царство Божие» → Царство Отца нашего (John 3:3,5). Keeps the Отц- stem; never Царство Божие here.
Standard Synodal spelling.
Standard Synodal spelling (John 3:28).
As in Synodal John 1:41 «Мессия»; used where the English keeps the transliterated title rather than Христос.
Standard Russian Bible term (Ветхий/Новый Завет). Distinct from договор (secular contract).

Renders Hebrew hesed (loyal covenant love). Synodal usually flattens hesed to «милость» (mercy), which loses the love; неизменная любовь restores the warmth and loyalty. REVIEWER TO CONFIRM vs «верная любовь» (faithful love), paralleling Telugu స్థిరమైన ప్రేమ.

Renders Hebrew racham (womb-love) / Greek splanchnizomai (gut-level tender mercy). Синодальный often uses the verb «сжалился»; the noun милосердие carries the inward tenderness. Alternative «сострадание» (suffering-with) available if the reviewer wants the visceral splanchnizomai sense foregrounded.
Standard Synodal term.
As in Synodal John 3:16 «жизнь вечную»; вечная жизнь is the equal modern word-order variant.
Follows Synodal «родится свыше» (John 3:3,7). Свыше preserves the deliberate John 3 double meaning 'from above / anew'; preferred over the flatter «рождён заново» (born anew only).
